experiment: Fork A — cross-model coordination CAPABILITY (possibility, not cost/value)
claim_type: capability / possibility — pigeon carries state across model boundaries that the chain cannot reconstruct without it. NOT a cost or value/need claim.
substrate: 3 separate agent CLIs, no shared memory: claude (Anthropic, architect) -> opencode/mimo-v2.5-free (to_wire) -> agy/Antigravity (from_wire). Genuinely cross-model.
repo: controlled minimal package `ledger` (an Account dataclass); appropriate for a capability demo (a controlled scenario, not a public OSS repo).

design.task: Implement to_wire/from_wire against a fixed wire CONTRACT (keys acct/cents/ts, ts=epoch-seconds int, omit note when None; lenient from_wire). The contract is ARBITRARY and the idiomatic Python defaults are its opposite.
design.off_disk_thread: The contract is given ONLY to hop 1 (architect) by prompt and never written into the code; nothing in the repo lets a downstream model infer it.
design.ablation: bridge = pigeon coordinate carries the architect's contract handoff to the implementers (receives pointer). no-bridge = same opencode->agy implementers, cold, no contract handoff, sharing only the filesystem (the realistic 'remove the bridge' baseline).
design.held_out_grader: accept.py (agents never see it): asserts the exact contract keys/types + round-trip + leniency. Validated to fail-pristine / pass-reference.
design.competence_control: Require bridge ~N/N (chain CAN do it -> rules out model weakness) AND no-bridge ~0/N with failures = contract violations (not crashes).

failure_mode_nobridge: Identical across all 5 trials: cold implementers produced working, internally-consistent code (to_wire+from_wire both present, mutually round-tripping) but used IDIOMATIC keys ['name','balance_cents','created','note'] with isoformat/strict parsing instead of the contract's ['acct','cents','ts'] epoch-int/lenient. The held-out test catches the contract violation. This is exactly the state pigeon's handoff carries and the bare filesystem does not.
verdict: CAPABILITY DEMONSTRATED. With pigeon's bridge the cross-model chain reproduces an off-disk contract 5/5; without it the same models default to incompatible idiomatic choices 0/5. Proves POSSIBILITY (the one thing pigeon does that nothing else does), not value or need.
caveats: Possibility, not value: this shows pigeon ENABLES cross-model state transfer; it does NOT show anyone needs a 3-model chain. (Pairs with the cost benchmark, which showed pigeon is token-neutral, not cheaper.)
Controlled scenario (synthetic `ledger` repo) — chosen for an airtight off-disk contract + held-out grader; could be replicated on a public OSS repo for grounding.
agy/Antigravity required two fixes to participate (see notes): a pigeon source fix (stdin=DEVNULL on runner spawn) + pure-edit task design (agy 1.0.10 cannot run shell commands under coordinate).
pigeon_fix_applied: src/pigeon/coordinate/__init__.py runner Popen now passes stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL (headless runners must not inherit/read stdin). Root cause of agy hanging; full pigeon suite green. UNCOMMITTED pending review.
